# Transcrição: 6. Why Do You Spend Money With

- **Fonte:** 6. Why Do You Spend Money With
- **Duração:** 02:05
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:05]

Okay, the next list, who do you spend money with? Man, this is easy. I want you just to make a list of people that you do business with. These people are obligated to you, okay? Like the laundromat that you get your clothes cleaned at. Where do you rent from? Okay? The bank you do business with. The mortgage broker that did the loan on your house. The real estate agent that sold the house to you. Where do you get your car insurance, your life insurance, the grocery store you go to, the stock broker you do business with? The guy at Merrill Lynch? He's a prospect. Where did you buy your telephone? The guy that sold you the phone that didn't know what he was doing and put you on a plan for two years and you thought you were getting a free phone that by the way cost you $2,400. That's what you paid for that phone. Why shouldn't you get some business from him for a change? Make him a prospect. How about where you bought your computer? Where you buy your clothes? Where your spouse buys their clothes? How about where you get your cigars from? Okay? I smoke cigars so that's on my list. The FedEx guy, the UPS guy, the postal guy. He's not going to buy a product or doesn't know other people. How about the people that contracted to do your house? Or how about the people that gave you a bid on doing some work for your kitchen? One simple way to do this, okay? And I just gave you a short list. I'm sure your list is bigger than this. Pull out a checkbook or credit card statement from the last 90 days or the last 12 months and look at everybody you gave money to. Build a file on them. We're not going to contact them yet. We're just going to build the file. This file is this. Anyone you've given money to or made a commitment to. Who could be on my list? American Airlines. Okay? I want you to think big here. American Airlines. I spent a lot of money with American Delta in United. Why don't I pick the senior vice president of those company, tell them how good a client I've been, for instance, Delta, 1 million miles I've flown with them. How much money have I spent with them over the last 10 years and then introduce them to my book? Huh. I'm getting creative with building my pipeline. That's what I want you to do here. Do not move on to the next chapter. Management. Do not let your people move on to the next chapter. And management, you make your own list as well. Everybody get involved in building up that prospect.
